project.project_title: Russia Desk - two-page Russian intelligence-and-history analytical desk
project.source_strategy: Use sources to convert regional knowledge into bounded analytic variables: actor interests, institutional constraints, strategic culture, deception patterns, force posture, and uncertainty limits.
project.distinctiveness_component_operation: Route one Russia source family into actor interest, institutional constraint, strategic culture, deception pattern, force posture, uncertainty limit, and analytic judgment.
project.distinctiveness_component_output: A bounded source-to-judgment chain with visible variables and explicit uncertainty.
project.acceptance_test: A reviewer can see why this project could not be swapped with another page without losing the mechanism.
project.best_improvement: Show how the desk converts regional knowledge into bounded analytic judgments rather than commentary.
project.build_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator

components.project_title: Russia Desk - two-page Russian intelligence-and-history analytical desk
components.build_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
components.input_fixture: books, journals, transcripts, maps, lecture notes, local pages, source markers, and visual dossiers
components.operation_to_perform: separates source families and exact-source candidates before strategic judgment; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
components.observable_output: admitted Russia source lane as a reviewable artifact
components.inspection_protocol: Show baseline AI output, structured human review, correction or constraint, confidence change, and release decision.
components.assigned_source: Russian Energy Policy and Military Power Putin's quest for greatness Pavel K. Baev
components.source_action: Extract the method rule that this component must obey, then add that rule to the acceptance criteria. Component under review: Russia Source Lane.
components.source_claim_ceiling: The source may guide structure and proof design from metadata routing, but public factual warrant requires close reading or already verified project evidence.
components.close_read_task: Close-read the assigned source for the exact rule, limitation, example, or mechanism that should alter Russia Source Lane.
components.acceptance_tests: The input, operation, and expected output are all visible.; The evidence locator is present or the missing evidence is explicitly named.; The result supports the component claim without implying broader completion.
components.falsification_rule: The component fails if human review is ornamental and does not change, narrow, block, or justify the output.
components.distinctiveness_pressure: A reviewer must see what structured human judgment changed in the AI-supported output.
components.advanced_component_to_add_if_weak: delta board comparing baseline, SAT-supported review, adjudication, and final claim
components.next_build_step: Trace one Russia claim through Russia Source Lane; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.
components.reviewer_question: Can the component show what human structured analysis changed in the AI output?
components.claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.

claim_boundary: This packet defines proof builds and capture protocols. It does not claim external validation unless the project record independently supports it.
